config_notes: Initial config for Concord, MA based on the Concord Zoning Bylaw. Concord is similar to neighboring Acton in scale and zoning structure but has partial sewer in West Concord/town-center. Revolutionary-era historic district covers a large fraction of the town center. Most parcels remain on private septic.

ma_adu_by_right.description: Affordable Homes Act §8: ADUs permitted by-right in single-family districts statewide. Up to 900 sqft or 50% of primary unit (whichever is less). No owner-occupancy requirement. Local site plan review allowed; no special permit / variance.
ma_adu_by_right.notes: Effective 2025-02-02 per Affordable Homes Act (Ch. 150 of the Acts of 2024). Local Concord Zoning Bylaw still requires updating but state law preempts.

parcel_data.notes: MassGIS Massachusetts_Property_Tax_Parcels FeatureServer. Concord TOWN_ID=67 (auto-discovered by name).

zoning_data.notes: Concord Zoning Bylaw PDF. Setbacks and ADU allowances per district below pending analyst verification.

assessor_data.notes: Assessor attributes (YEAR_BUILT, BLD_AREA, USE_CODE, etc.) included in MassGIS L3 layer.

sewer_service_notes: Concord has partial public sewer covering West Concord and parts of Concord Center; most of the town remains on private septic. This config flags sewer_service=false as the conservative default — analyst override per parcel may be needed for in-sewer-area lots.

RA.label: Residence A (40,000 sqft)
RA.adu_notes: ADU permitted by-right under MA AHA §8. Concord's local bylaw historically required special permit; state law now preempts. Detached structures subject to standard setbacks.
citations: Concord Zoning Bylaw §4.4 Residence A District

RAA.label: Residence AA (60,000 sqft)
RAA.adu_notes: ADU permitted by-right under MA AHA §8. Larger-lot rural district — siting flexibility is high but setback footprint is large.
citations: Concord Zoning Bylaw §4.5 Residence AA District

RB.label: Residence B (in-town)
RB.adu_notes: Mixed single- and two-family, in-town residential. AHA §8 applies. Among the better Plinth deployment zones in Concord — moderate lot sizes with lower setbacks.
citations: Concord Zoning Bylaw §4.6 Residence B District

RC.label: Residence C (compact in-town)
RC.adu_notes: Most permissive Concord residential zone. AHA §8 applies. Highest coverage allowance and smallest setbacks — best Plinth deployment fit despite small lot sizes.
citations: Concord Zoning Bylaw §4.7 Residence C District

septic_assumptions.notes: Massachusetts Title 5 (310 CMR 15.00) governs all private systems. SSURGO soil suitability is the dominant signal — much of Concord sits on Paxton/Woodbridge glacial-till hardpan rated 'Very limited' for conventional septic. Innovative-alternative systems may be feasible but rarely cost-effective for ADU-scale loads.

parking_assumptions.notes: MA AHA §8 caps ADU parking minimum at 1 space, 0 within 1/2 mile of MBTA station. Concord has commuter rail (Concord and West Concord stations) — parcels within walk distance qualify for the 0-space minimum.

overlays.label: FEMA Flood Zone AE
overlays.notes: Concord River and Sudbury River floodplains cover significant land area on the eastern and southern edges of town.

overlays.label: Wetlands Buffer (100ft)
overlays.notes: MA Wetlands Protection Act 100-ft buffer around any vegetated or bordering vegetated wetland.

overlays.label: Concord Center Local Historic District
overlays.notes: Concord Historic Districts Commission review required for any exterior change. Multiple LHDs cover the town center, Monument Square, and Walden-Heywood. Reviews take 30–60 days and typically constrain materials/finishes.

overlays.label: Rivers Protection Act (200ft)
overlays.notes: Concord, Sudbury, and Assabet Rivers all trigger 200-ft Riverfront Area review under the MA Rivers Protection Act.

overlays.label: Water Resource Protection District
overlays.notes: Concord Water Resource Protection District restricts impervious cover and septic siting near public water supplies.
